V-M-FOUNDATION-SETTINGS.evidence: Settings frozen, env-prefix AISW_, defaults for log_level/workspace/db URLs.

commands.cmd: uv run pytest tests/unit/test_logging_setup.py tests/unit/test_logging_traced.py tests/unit/test_logging_events_catalog.py tests/unit/test_logging_events_chunk2.py -q

V-M-FOUNDATION-LOGGING.evidence: structlog JSON renderer, ISO UTC timestamps, correlation_id contextvar bound/reset (chunk-1). @traced decorator emits <prefix>.{start,done,error} with duration_ms; never inspects args/kwargs/return (PII-safe). logging_events.py SSoT catalog of snake_case dotted event keys covers chunk-1 entrypoints and chunk-2 anchors (scheduler.job.*, storage.slow_query, claude_cli.*). CorrelationMiddleware binds correlation_id + telegram_id + chat_id + update_id into structlog.contextvars for one Update lifetime; autouse contextvars cleanup fixture between tests. PII redactor passes through chunk-2 metadata fields unchanged while still redacting free-text fields on the same record (idempotent).

V-M-AUTH-USERS.evidence: users.toml parsed/validated; allowlist atomic swap; SIGHUP+watchdog 500ms debounce; sha256 short-circuit.

V-M-STORAGE.evidence: WAL, synchronous=NORMAL, foreign_keys=ON, busy_timeout=5000 applied per connection. Chunk-2 slow-query listener attached per AsyncEngine in build_engine() of jobs/audit/sessions: emits storage.slow_query (WARNING) when one cursor execute exceeds settings.storage_slow_query_threshold_ms (default 200). Fields: db_name, statement_sha8 (sha256[:8] of raw SQL string), duration_ms. NEVER logs raw SQL or parameter values.

V-M-STORAGE-JOBS.evidence: jobs/tracker_answers/jobs_dlq schema via alembic baseline; JobPayload discriminated union round-trips (parse_job_payload); PRAGMAs verified on engine. aisw-269 (Phase-D.b.2b): DigestPayload.wiki_scope widened Literal['all'] → 'all' | list[str] (non-empty list) — DigestPayload(wiki_scope='all') and DigestPayload(wiki_scope=['health']) both validate; wiki_scope=[] rejected; model_dump(mode='json') ↔ parse_job_payload round-trips the list shape; an existing {'kind':'digest','wiki_scope':'all',...} dict still validates (no jobs.db migration; wiki_scope is not the union discriminator). VERSION 0.0.4 → 0.0.5. aisw-xi8 (classifier v2, Phase-B, resolved Q2/DEC-6): two ADDITIVE JobPayload union members — RecurringReminderPayload(kind='recurring_reminder', message:str, recurrence:Recurrence, category:Literal['medication','event','generic']='generic') and CheckInPayload(kind='check_in', question_topic:str, recurrence:Recurrence, wiki_id:str|None=None) — both validate via parse_job_payload and round-trip through model_dump(mode='json'); extra='forbid'+frozen kept; the existing 5 kinds (wiki_run/digest/cron_user/purge/reminder_job) still validate unchanged (FR-15, no jobs.db migration — additive discriminator tag); an unrecognised/legacy dict without a 'kind' matching either new Literal still raises the pre-existing union_tag_invalid, proving the widening is non-breaking. VERSION 0.0.7 → 0.0.8.

V-M-STORAGE-AUDIT.evidence: 10 tables present per D-006 amendment (alembic baseline); PRAGMAs verified; append-only invariants. chat_log (D-033, aisw-kml): redact_chat_text drops the denylist (sk-ant-/Bearer/password=) before write; ChatLogWriter.write_in/write_out persist one row per turn; read_recent_window returns the last-20 / 24h ChatTurns oldest-first for the router/Stage-0 buffer.

V-M-STORAGE-SESSIONS.evidence: users/pending_users/pending_confirms/inbox_hint_cache/fsm/user_digest_prefs/user_active_wiki tables (alembic baseline + alembic/sessions/0002 + 0004 stepwise; test_baselines: stamp 0003_cards_enabled → `upgrade head` creates user_active_wiki); PRAGMAs verified. user_active_wiki (aisw-0ym): ActiveWikiPointer.set_active upserts the per-telegram_id row; get_active returns the name only within ACTIVE_WIKI_TTL_HOURS (24h), else None (stale ⇒ no force-route). user_digest_prefs (aisw-pv8): get_digest_prefs → DigestPrefs(True,True) when no users row or no prefs row; set_digest_section upserts one bool (creates the row with defaults if absent, bumps updated_at_utc), no-op + defaults for an unknown telegram_id, ValueError on an unknown section; deleting the users row CASCADE-removes the prefs row; TOGGLEABLE_DIGEST_SECTIONS ⊆ EXPAND_SECTION_KEYS and SECTION_DISPLAY_NAME keys == TOGGLEABLE_DIGEST_SECTIONS. test_baselines: stamp 0001_sessions_baseline then `alembic upgrade head` → user_digest_prefs present (0002's idempotent Base.metadata.create_all delta), and `upgrade head` from empty also creates it (baseline create_all).

commands.cmd: uv run pytest tests/unit/test_runtime_wiring.py tests/unit/test_router_adapter.py tests/unit/test_librarian_adapter.py tests/unit/test_main_hint_catalog.py tests/unit/test_main_runner_adapter.py tests/unit/test_main_digest_adapter.py -q
uv run python -m ai_steward_wiki # manual smoke with AISW_TG_BOT_TOKEN_LOCAL

V-M-RUNTIME-WIRING.evidence: Unit tests cover sync-URL derivation, data-dir creation, users.toml load fallbacks, signal handler wiring, _amain composition with mocks, missing-token fail-fast, main → asyncio.run. aisw-o6m (ADR-034): test_main_runner_adapter.py — a confident WIKI_QUERY match runs wiki_path/wiki_id scoped to the matched WIKI with no layouts block in the overlay (CLAUDE.md arrives via assemble_prompt); an ambiguous match runs cross-root at the user root with "Карта WIKI пользователя" + every WIKI's Data-layout excerpt in the overlay; a hint_catalog_resolver raising degrades to cross-root with the legacy placeholder overlay (no exception propagates); WEB_TASK never calls either resolver and keeps legacy behaviour; an adapter constructed without the two resolvers (back-compat) also keeps legacy cross-root behaviour. test_main_digest_adapter.py — a full-digest call (section=None) with extra_add_dirs appends the layouts block (header + one "## <stem>" section per extra WIKI) to planner_context before user_input; a section-expand call (/expand) with the same extra_add_dirs never injects layouts. aisw-5sd: make_hint_catalog_resolver — telegram_id → {wiki_stem: hint_text} via surrogate_id_of + owner_wikis_resolver + get_or_refresh_hint per domain CLAUDE.md; unknown sender (surrogate None) → {} (fast-path falls through); a domain with no '## Inbox hint' → omitted; no domain WIKIs → {} without touching the cache (get_or_refresh_hint monkeypatched in the unit). aisw-hcl: VoiceHandler/PhotoIngestor wired into DefaultPipeline; media _staging sweep job registered; _WikiRunnerAdapter.run promotes staged media into <wiki>/raw/media after a successful run. aisw-dsg: _RouterAdapter — ensure_inbox_wiki, _render_raw_sidecar, run_wiki_session in Inbox-WIKI/ with prompts/inbox.md, parse_router_reply, WikiRunnerError→RouterError, inbox.router.* markers. aisw-zd9: _LibrarianAdapter — resolve_target_wiki (create new dir; ROUTE-missing → create + warning; cap → rejected; bad name → rejected), stage_raw_into_wiki (raw/<ts>_text.md + promote media), run_wiki_session with wiki_id=f"{tid}/<Name>" + overlay=domain-default.md + timeout_s=None, IngestOutcome(ok|run_failed|rejected), WikiRunnerError→run_failed keeps the raw, inbox.route.* markers via capsys. Manual smoke: `python -m ai_steward_wiki` starts, logs runtime.media_pipeline.wired + polling.start, Ctrl-C → shutdown.done, exit 0. aisw-xi8 (classifier v2, Phase-C.1, DEC-5): test_main_runner_adapter.py's confident/ambiguous/degraded/WEB_TASK/back-compat cases (aisw-o6m) are re-expressed against the new taxonomy — a confident (intent=WIKI, action="query") match still runs scoped with no layouts block; an ambiguous (intent=WIKI, action="query") match still runs cross-root with the layouts block; (intent=WEB) still keeps legacy behaviour and never calls either resolver; the back-compat case (adapter constructed without the two resolvers) is UNCHANGED. All assertions on the OLD `Intent.WIKI_QUERY`/`Intent.WEB_TASK` enum members are migrated per the DEC-14 mapping table (Phase-C.4) to the (intent, action) tuple form.

commands.cmd: uv run pytest tests/unit/tg/test_handlers.py tests/unit/tg/test_commands.py tests/unit/tg/test_digest_sections.py tests/unit/tg/test_pipeline.py tests/unit/tg/test_pipeline_document.py -q

V-M-TG-HANDLERS-WIRING.evidence: Unit tests cover callback parsing (8 variants), router/dispatcher wiring (7 message handlers: text/voice/photo/audio/video_note/document/callback), _download_bytes, text/voice/photo/audio/video_note/document/confirm dispatch with fake aiogram Message+CallbackQuery, L1 dedup short-circuit, photo→runner(media_paths)→deliver, photo+caption, photo L2 dedup, image-document routed, STT-unavailable graceful path, fallback acks when voice/photo handlers absent. aisw-hcl (media handling): F.audio + F.video_note route to on_voice; photo handler forwards message.caption; on_photo runs Claude vision via the wiki pipeline. aisw-269 (Phase-D.b.2b — the bot's first slash commands): a dedicated Command('digest_now')/Command('expand') router registered BEFORE the F.text catch-all — a non-command text message still reaches MessagePipeline.on_text unchanged (router-order regression test). /digest_now: select the calling owner's jobs.Job rows kind=='digest_job' status=='scheduled' → for each call scheduler.firing.fire_digest_job(job_id); 0 rows → ru "no digest configured" hint (tg.command.digest_now.empty); a per-job exception is caught and logged (tg.command.digest_now.job_failed) so the remaining jobs still run; tg.command.digest_now.done with n_jobs. /expand <section>: arg ∈ {today, meds, trackers, wiki} else ru usage line (tg.command.expand.bad_section); resolve the owner WIKI set (Inbox excluded) — empty → ru notice; else digest_runner(wiki_id=primary, …, section=<key>) → reply via send_message (no run_outputs row); empty model text → ru "nothing for this section" line; tg.command.expand.delivered with section/chars; any exception at the handler boundary → generic ru error + tg.command.expand.failed. Both handlers wrap their body in try/except so nothing bubbles to the aiogram dispatcher. aisw-pv8 (Phase-D.b.2c — /digest_sections): Command('digest_sections') registered in the same commands group → firing.get_owner_digest_prefs(owner) → reply ru header + an inline keyboard (one button per TOGGLEABLE_DIGEST_SECTIONS key: text "<ru label>: вкл ✅"/"… выкл ⬜", callback_data "digestsec:<key>:<0|1>" carrying the TARGET state) + tg.command.digest_sections.shown; DigestNotInitialisedError → ru unavailable line. digestsec: callback (@router.callback_query F.data startswith "digestsec:"): parse_digestsec_callback("digestsec:<section>:<0|1>") → (section, target) — None on any malformed/foreign-prefix/unknown-section/bad-flag/wrong-arity data → cb.answer + tg.command.digest_sections.bad_callback, no DB write; valid → firing.set_owner_digest_section(owner, section=, enabled=target) → cb.message.edit_reply_markup with the rebuilt keyboard (the edit is wrapped — TelegramBadRequest "not modified" on a stale tap is swallowed, tg.command.digest_sections.edit_skipped — the DB write already succeeded) → cb.answer + tg.command.digest_sections.toggled{owner_telegram_id, section, enabled}; re-tapping a button whose state already matches is idempotent. parse_digestsec_callback unit-tested over ok + reject variants; handler-level tests use the existing aiogram test harness (firing accessors monkeypatched), mirroring test_commands.py. The new command is in the same commands-router group registered before F.text, so a plain text message still reaches MessagePipeline.on_text (router-order regression still holds).

commands.cmd: uv run pytest tests/unit/tg/test_pipeline_classifier_wiring.py tests/unit/tg/test_pipeline_router.py tests/unit/tg/test_pipeline_hint_fastpath.py tests/unit/tg/test_pipeline_route_ingest.py tests/unit/tg/test_pipeline_route_confirm.py tests/unit/tg/test_pipeline_confirm_callback.py tests/unit/tg/test_pipeline_chat_log.py tests/unit/tg/test_pipeline_active_wiki.py tests/unit/tg/test_pipeline_subthreshold.py tests/unit/tg/test_pipeline_catalog_routing.py tests/unit/tg/test_pipeline_protocol_widening.py tests/unit/tg/test_pipeline_job_manage.py tests/unit/tg/test_pipeline_job_create.py tests/unit/wiki/test_runner_aggregate.py -q

V-M-TG-PIPELINE-CLASSIFIER.evidence: Unit tests cover text happy path, L1 dup short-circuit, L2 dedup hit, classifier error → ACK_CLASSIFY_ERR_RU, runner error → ACK_RUNNER_ERR_RU, empty runner output → ACK_TEXT_RU via deliver, back-compat None injection, voice happy/empty/back-compat, structlog markers via capsys; aggregate_text across stream-json payload shapes. aisw-dsg: routable intents with a wired Router go through router.route(); router=None / non-routable use the legacy path; RouterError → ACK_RUNNER_ERR_RU; tg.pipeline.router.* markers. aisw-zd9: the ROUTE/CREATE_WIKI librarian.ingest() path (now reached via the confirm callback). aisw-e45 (Phase-C): a ROUTE/CREATE_WIKI decision (wired librarian + output) → no immediate ingest; ConfirmationService.request_explicit called with category="route_ingest" + build_route_confirm_keyboard + a draft round-trippable via route_action_from_payload; recap mentions target + notes; CLARIFY/REJECT and librarian=None → send_message(decision.notes), no confirm row. on_confirm_callback: route_ingest row + resolve→"confirmed" → ROUTE_CONFIRM_ACK_RU then librarian.ingest() with the reconstructed decision + media Paths → status="ok" → output.deliver(reply) else send_message(reply); resolve→"cancelled" → ROUTE_CONFIRM_CANCELLED_RU, no ingest; resolve→None → ROUTE_CONFIRM_STALE_RU, no ingest; non-route_ingest or missing row → legacy resolve path (tg.pipeline.confirm); tg.pipeline.route.confirm_* + confirm.route_dispatched markers via capsys. Integration (RUN_INTEGRATION=1): test_e2e_pipeline.py scenario 6 — routable text → recap + route_ingest pending row (no domain WIKI yet) → on_confirm_callback("confirm") → real Stage-1b librarian writes into the target <Domain>-WIKI/raw/, ROUTE_CONFIRM_ACK_RU sent, reply via output.deliver; tolerant of CLARIFY/REJECT. aisw-269 (Phase-D.b.2b — named-subset WIKI in the digest fast-path): after parse_recurrence, _extract_wiki_names(turn_text, owner_wiki_stems) — whole-token, case-insensitive intersection with the owner's *-WIKI/ dir-stems; all mentioned names resolve → list[str], none mentioned → 'all', a name-shaped token that does not resolve → return None → ru clarification (lists the owner's WIKI names), NO confirm draft built, NO job created (tg.pipeline.digest.wiki_unknown); the category='digest' confirm draft carries wiki_scope (the new shape) and the recap/ack names the chosen WIKIs when scoped; on confirm → create_digest_job(... wiki_scope=...). Unit: «сводка по Health каждый день в 9» with the owner having Health-WIKI/ → draft.wiki_scope==['Health'], recap names it; «сводка по Money …» with no Money-WIKI/ → clarification, no draft; no names mentioned → wiki_scope=='all' (today's behaviour). aisw-5sd (Phase-E.b — '## Inbox hint' router-bypass): a new pre-router block (between the reminder fast-path and the routable branch) guarded on intent ∈ _ROUTABLE_INTENTS ∧ router ∧ hint_catalog_resolver ∧ librarian ∧ output — builds the sender's hint catalog (hint_catalog_resolver), score_catalog(text, catalog); on is_confident → RouterDecision(intent=ROUTE, target_wiki=<stem>, raw="", parsed_ok=True), WITHOUT awaiting router.route; not confident → tg.pipeline.hint_fastpath.miss(reason=ambiguous if top_score≥MIN_SCORE else no_match) + fallthrough → heavy router runs; empty catalog → miss(reason=empty_catalog) + fallthrough, scorer not consulted; hint_catalog_resolver=None or librarian=None ⇒ block skipped, heavy router runs. aisw-2ra (silent auto-route): a confident match now routes SILENTLY (D-023 'auto') — _ingest_and_deliver(decision) runs the Stage-1b librarian.ingest immediately (no Confirm/Cancel keyboard); on status=="ok" the active-WIKI pointer is set, the reply delivered, then either ConfirmationService.request_explicit(category="route_ingest", build_route_redirect_keyboard) posts a "✅ Записал в <WIKI>" ack with a one-tap redirect picker of the owner's OTHER WIKIs (tapping reuses on_wikipick_callback) OR — when there is no other WIKI — ConfirmationService.auto_ack(ROUTE_SILENT_ACK_NOREDIR_RU); a non-ok ingest sends the composed reply via send_message and offers no redirect. _ingest_and_deliver is the single Stage-1b ingest+deliver tail shared with _handle_route_confirm. tg.pipeline.hint_fastpath.silent_route marker carries target_wiki/score/margin/status/run_id. Unit (test_pipeline_hint_fastpath.py, capsys for markers): confident hit routes silently with a redirect picker (target WIKI excluded), no-other-WIKI → plain auto_ack, failed ingest → error send + no redirect; ambiguous/no_match/empty/disabled all fall through to router.route; hint_fastpath.{catalog,silent_route,miss,fallthrough} markers asserted on their paths. Loader label (FR-2) deferred to aisw-05k. aisw-xi8 (classifier v2, Phase-C.1..C.3 — supersedes the v1 REMINDER/DIGEST/SMALLTALK-shaped tests above per the DEC-14 mapping, migrated in Phase-C.4): _run_text_pipeline is now a flat ordered if/elif over 6 intents (wiki|job|web|chat|admin|unknown), each a thin call into _handle_<intent> (DEC-1). test_pipeline_subthreshold.py: immediately after tg.pipeline.classify.done, confidence<CLASSIFIER_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD(0.85) on intent∈{job,admin} → ru clarification sent + tg.pipeline.subthreshold.clarify logged + handler returns WITHOUT reaching any handler/runner (asserted via a spy — the generic root runner is never invoked, closing #78/#96 structurally, DEC-2/FR-10); the SAME low confidence on intent∈{wiki,web,chat,unknown} proceeds normally (non-destructive intents are exempt); confidence≥threshold on job/admin proceeds normally. test_pipeline_catalog_routing.py: wiki/catalog with action=None or action='catalog' → routed to the existing Stage-1a RouterIntent.LIST_WIKIS path (zero router changes, FR-11 — closes the measured 99/100 "Покажи мои вики" miss); wiki/query and wiki/lint → the generic answer runner (streaming tail); the routable predicate is asserted as a function over (intent, action), not a static frozenset membership test (DEC-3). test_pipeline_protocol_widening.py: a WikiRunner/StreamingDelivery double constructed WITHOUT threading `action` still satisfies the Protocol (action defaults to None) — proves the DEC-5 widening is backward-compatible; a double asserting on the received `action` value confirms wiki/query threads action='query' and all other intents thread action=None (or their own action value, e.g. job/web/chat/admin/unknown pass through whatever JobSlots/WikiSlots resolved, if any). test_pipeline_job_manage.py (DEC-9/DEC-10, Phase-C.2): job/list renders scheduler.manage.list_owner_jobs' output (tg.pipeline.job.list); job/cancel and job/reschedule with EXACTLY 1 needle match build a destructive 'job_cancel' Confirm/Cancel draft (FR-8) or a validated reschedule draft; >1 match builds a 'job_pick' numbered inline keyboard (jobpick:<pending_id>:<idx>) with tg.pipeline.job.pick_requested; 0 matches → ru "not found" + rendered list, NO pending row (tg.pipeline.job.not_found); confirming a job_cancel/job_pick draft calls scheduler.manage.cancel_job/reschedule_once/reschedule_recurring + ru ack (tg.pipeline.job.cancel / tg.pipeline.job.reschedule); cancel/stale → ru notice, no mutation. test_pipeline_job_create.py (DEC-11, Phase-C.3): job/create kind=once and kind=digest reach the EXACT SAME reminder/digest confirm-draft builders and on_confirm_callback handlers as before aisw-xi8 (regression-asserted byte-identical downstream behaviour — only the entry point changed from regex-detected intent to classifier slots, FR-4/FR-7); kind=recurring and kind=check_in build NEW 'job_recurring'/'job_checkin' confirm drafts via parse_recurrence + humanize_recurrence, and on confirm call scheduler.firing.create_recurring_job / scheduler.cron_user.create_check_in_job respectively. All 4 new confirm categories (job_cancel, job_pick, job_recurring, job_checkin) share ONE _handle_job_confirm dispatch disambiguated by draft.category (one anchor family tg.pipeline.job.confirm_{requested,created,cancelled,stale} — KISS/SSoT over 4 near-duplicate handlers, per _code-quality review); the pre-existing categories route_ingest|reminder|digest keep their exact strings and handlers so in-flight pending rows survive the deploy restart (R-3, unit-tested with a persisted old-category row still dispatching correctly). The classifying Python forks (_RECURRING_KEYWORDS + punt, _DIGEST_DISABLE_RE/_DIGEST_RESCHEDULE_RE/_detect_digest_action) are asserted ABSENT from the module (a grep-based regression test in test_pipeline_catalog_routing.py or a dedicated smoke test fails if any of these symbols are re-introduced, FR-2).

V-M-TG-PIPELINE-STREAMING.evidence: 7 unit tests cover fast path (no placeholder, single deliver), slow path (placeholder + buffered+live feeds + final aggregate), runner exception (finalize editor + ACK_RUNNER_ERR_RU via sender), back-compat None injection, aggregate_text-as-truth, outcome.text fallback, empty-outcome ACK_TEXT_RU fallback. 3 unit tests cover run_wiki_session on_event callback (per-event delivery, exception swallow+log, no-callback back-compat). Full suite 385/385 green; make lint and grace lint clean. aisw-xi8 (classifier v2, Phase-C.1, DEC-5): run_and_deliver's new `action: str | None = None` param is threaded straight through to the wrapped runner.run(action=action) unchanged — covered by tests/unit/tg/test_pipeline_protocol_widening.py (shared with M-TG-PIPELINE-CLASSIFIER since both Protocols live in pipeline.py); no change to the race/placeholder/stream-editing mechanics.

V-M-CLAUDE-CLI-COMMON.evidence: resolve_binary (/-path short-circuit + shutil.which), build_env (CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR + minimal PATH only), neutral_cwd (no project CLAUDE.md auto-discovery), system_prompt_argv (inlines prompt file content), truncate_stderr (UTF-8 decode + length cap).

commands.cmd: uv run pytest tests/unit/auth/test_onboarding.py tests/unit/auth/test_admin.py tests/unit/auth/test_lint_onboarding.py -q

V-M-ONBOARD-ADMIN.evidence: pending_users idempotent upsert + TTL purge; intro template slug validation (REQUIRED_SLUGS, lint_onboarding); AdminService approve/reject/elevate/demote with admin gating (NotAnAdmin) + admin_events trail (D-028); ShadowEmitter default LoggingShadowEmitter.

V-M-SCHEDULER.evidence: build_scheduler (AsyncIOScheduler + SQLAlchemyJobStore, D-003); PriorityJobQueue 5 lanes FIFO tiebreak; WikiLockManager 3-tier acquire/release + stale-PID recovery; classify_exception taxonomy + FailureCounter 3-strikes auto-disable (D-019); move_to_dlq writes jobs_dlq; kill_with_sequence SIGTERM→grace→SIGKILL (D-021); register_all_retention_jobs wires purge-pending + media _staging sweep + retention.

commands.cmd: uv run pytest tests/unit/scheduler/test_firing.py tests/unit/scheduler/test_firing_recurring.py tests/unit/tg/test_pipeline_reminder.py tests/unit/tg/test_pipeline_digest.py tests/unit/tg/test_digest_e2e.py -q

V-M-SCHEDULER-FIRING.evidence: Pipeline reminder fast-path (BEFORE the Stage-1a router): intent==REMINDER & confidence≥THRESHOLD + future time → ConfirmationService.request_explicit with category='reminder' draft (when_utc, message, lead_time_min) + 2-button keyboard, no Job row yet; message resolution = distilled_payload['reminder_text'] if non-empty str else raw text; prefer_future=True rolls a bare past wall-clock time forward; explicitly-past absolute date → ru rejection, no confirm; escalate → ru clarification, no confirm; recurring-digest phrasing → ru "not yet", no confirm. on_confirm_callback category=='reminder' + resolve→'confirmed' → create_reminder_job writes a jobs.Job(kind='reminder_job', payload round-trips through parse_job_payload, scheduled_at_utc==when_utc) + scheduler.add_job once with a DateTrigger at when_utc, id=='reminder:<id>', misfire_grace_time is None; ack sent. cancel→ru cancelled, no Job row; resolve→None→ru stale; double-confirm idempotent. firing.fire_job: context set + Job pending → sender.send_message(chat_id, '🔔 Напоминание: <message>') + row done; Job not pending → skipped, no send; send raises → row failed + last_error + scheduler.reminder.deliver_failed; context not set → RuntimeError. ReminderPayload in parse_job_payload: valid → ReminderPayload, extra key → ValidationError. Integration: reminder message → request_explicit → simulate confirm → jobs.jobs row with kind='reminder_job' & matching scheduled_at_utc → invoke fire_job(job_id) with a fake sender → one send_message + row done. Known limitation (documented, no MVP reconciliation): a crash between the Job commit and scheduler.add_job leaves a pending row with no trigger → silent miss. (aisw-kcz; D-002/D-010/D-022; tech-spec §3/§6; ADR-006.) Digest path (aisw-oqq, Phase-D.b.1): pipeline digest fast-path (replaces the reminder branch's recurring-keyword stub): recurring-digest phrasing → parse_recurrence; on escalate → ru clarification, no confirm; else → ConfirmationService.request_explicit with category='digest' draft (recurrence serialised, wiki_scope='all', window_hours=24) + 2-button keyboard, no Job row yet. on_confirm_callback category=='digest' + resolve→'confirmed' → create_digest_job writes a jobs.Job(kind='digest_job', status='scheduled', priority==Lane.DIGEST, payload round-trips through parse_job_payload) + scheduler.add_job once with a CronTrigger (kwargs == recurrence.to_cron(), timezone == recurrence.tz), id=='digest:<id>', replace_existing=True; ack sent. cancel→ru cancelled, no Job row; resolve→None→ru stale; double-confirm idempotent. firing.fire_digest_job: context set + Job 'scheduled' → resolve owner WIKI set (minus Inbox-WIKI) → run_wiki_session called once with extra_add_dirs == the non-primary WIKI paths, under the Semaphore + WikiLockManager(primary), timeout_s==600 → assistant text (or the ru empty-digest line) delivered via tg/output (send_message or send_document); row stays status='scheduled', retry_count==0, finished_at_utc set. Job not 'scheduled' → skipped, no run. Owner has no *-WIKI → friendly ru line, finished, no strike. run_wiki_session raises (WikiRunnerError / WikiRunnerTimeoutError) → retry_count++; third consecutive failure → status='disabled' + scheduler.remove_job('digest:<id>') called + jobs_dlq row written; scheduler.digest.failed logged with error_class/retry_count/disabled. Bad/parse-failing payload → status='disabled' + DLQ immediately. context not set → DigestNotInitialisedError. DigestPayload in parse_job_payload: valid (with a Recurrence) → DigestPayload, extra key → ValidationError. run_wiki_session: extra_add_dirs paths appear in argv immediately after the primary '--add-dir <wiki_path>'; None → unchanged argv. e2e (in-process slow unit, mirrors the reminder e2e): recurring-digest text → request_explicit → simulate confirm → jobs.jobs row kind='digest_job' status='scheduled' + add_job with a CronTrigger → set_digest_context + fire_digest_job(job_id) with a fake runner-adapter (canned assistant events) → one delivery through tg/output → row still 'scheduled', retry_count==0. (aisw-oqq; D-002/D-019/D-024/D-025; tech-spec §3; ADR-007.) Digest interactive (aisw-269, Phase-D.b.2b): DigestRunner Protocol gains section:str|None=None — fire_digest_job still calls runner(...) with the default (full digest), byte-identical. fire_digest_job after resolving the owner WIKI set, when payload.wiki_scope is a list[str]: keep only (wid, path) whose wid (case-insensitive) ∈ the list → run_wiki_session gets the filtered set; scheduler.digest.scope_filter logged requested/kept/vanished; empty kept list → ru "all scoped WIKIs vanished" notice via send_message, finished_at_utc set, status stays 'scheduled' (NO strike — config drift, not a run failure), scheduler.digest.delivered logged empty='scope_vanished'. payload.wiki_scope=='all' → unchanged. create_digest_job accepts wiki_scope:str|list[str] and persists DigestPayload(wiki_scope=…); scheduler.digest.scheduled carries wiki_scope. Unit: fire_digest_job with wiki_scope=['health'] runs only that WIKI; vanished name → ru notice, no strike, status 'scheduled', scope_filter marker; create_digest_job(wiki_scope=['health','money']) persists a list shape round-trippable via parse_job_payload. (aisw-269; D-024; ADR-025.) Digest section toggles (aisw-pv8, Phase-D.b.2c): set_digest_context gains a sessions sessionmaker (the 7th context element). fire_digest_job, after building the planner context and before the runner call: get_digest_prefs(sessions_sm, owner_telegram_id) wrapped in try/except — any failure → scheduler.digest.prefs_read_failed + DigestPrefs(True,True) (degrade-to-all-on, never skip the digest); if prefs.disabled_keys is non-empty → planner_context += "\n\nНе включай разделы: <ru labels>." and scheduler.digest.sections_filtered{job_id, owner_telegram_id, disabled} logged; if empty → planner_context byte-identical to today (regression-guarded: no-prefs path calls the runner with the same string). runner(..., section=None) unchanged; the DigestRunner Protocol, _DigestRunnerAdapter, and run_section_expand are untouched (/expand ignores the toggle). get_owner_digest_prefs / set_owner_digest_section accessors (read the sessions sessionmaker from the digest context) → DigestNotInitialisedError when unwired; unit-tested in test_firing.py alongside the directive-injection cases (no-prefs byte-identical, trackers-off directive + sections_filtered marker, both-off lists both, prefs-read-failure degrades). (aisw-pv8; D-024; ADR-025/ADR-026.) Recurring reminder firing (aisw-xi8, Phase-B, DEC-6/DEC-7): create_recurring_job INSERTs a jobs.Job(kind='recurring_reminder', payload=RecurringReminderPayload) then registers a CronTrigger id=f'recurring:{id}', replace_existing=True (scheduler.recurring.scheduled); commit-before-add_job (same crash-window caveat as the reminder path, documented not reconciled for MVP). fire_recurring_job (picklable int): loads Job, guards an enabled/scheduled status; sends payload.message via TgSender.send_message VERBATIM — byte-identical every fire, NO Claude/LLM call (NFR-2 asserted: the fake runner/backend is never invoked on this path); job stays enabled after a successful send (NO terminal status transition, unlike fire_job's once-semantics — scheduler.recurring.fired + scheduler.recurring.delivered); 3 CONSECUTIVE send failures → status='disabled' + scheduler.remove_job('recurring:<id>') + move_to_dlq (scheduler.recurring.deliver_failed per failure, scheduler.recurring.auto_disabled on the 3rd — mirrors fire_digest_job's FailureCounter shape); a single failure followed by a success resets the streak (no premature disable). test_firing_recurring.py: happy-path verbatim delivery (message text asserted char-for-char against payload.message, including any embedded punctuation/emoji), 2 failures + 1 success does NOT disable, 3 consecutive failures DOES disable + DLQ + remove_job called exactly once. fire_job (once-semantics) is explicitly NOT reused/generalized for the recurring path — three similar ~15-line functions were judged simpler than one parametrized generalisation (KISS, DEC-7).

V-M-CLASSIFIER-STAGE0.evidence: classify orchestrates backend call + Pydantic validation + audit prompt_versions upsert + structlog; PromptCache semver+sha256 with reload(); parse_time dateparser→Haiku-fallback→escalate (D-010); ClaudeCliBackend resolves binary + JSON-mode envelope; AnthropicApiBackend optional; FakeClaudeRunner deterministic; INV-6 settings guard; ClassifierTimeoutError/SchemaError taxonomy. aisw-xi8 (classifier v2, Phase-A, FR-1/DEC-4): Intent is now the closed 6-member enum wiki|job|web|chat|admin|unknown (superseding the v1 9-member reminder/wiki_ingest/wiki_query/wiki_lint/digest/web_task/smalltalk/admin/unknown list per the DEC-14 mapping table); the "unknown" fallback value and stage0.py's retry-once-before-fallback policy (aisw-l3h) are unchanged by the rename. test_slots.py: WikiSlots(action=None) is the default and is valid (covers the measured 99/100 "Покажи мои вики" miss — an empty action still routes correctly downstream, DEC-3); WikiSlots(action='catalog'|'ingest'|'query'|'lint') all valid; JobSlots defaults to action='create'/kind='once' when the payload supplies neither; every JobSlots free-text slot (time_expr/schedule_expr/text/needle) round-trips verbatim — no whitespace/case normalisation, no translation (FR-12). parse_slots(model, distilled_payload): a well-formed dict validates into the model; an ill-formed dict (wrong-typed field, e.g. action=123) returns a DEFAULT instance (never raises) + logs classifier.slots.invalid with the offending model name — the caller never sees a ValidationError propagate to the user (Fail-Fast-with-graceful-default, DEC-4); unknown/extra keys in distilled_payload are silently ignored (forward-compatible with future slot additions). ClassifierResult shape is unchanged — distilled_payload stays dict[str,Any] (FR-1); no schema migration for the audit prompt_versions table.

V-M-CLASSIFIER-REGRESS.evidence: MANDATORY MANUAL gate before any prompts/classifier.md commit (FR-13, resolved Q4/DEC-13) — deliberately NOT wired into total-test/CI (100 real Haiku calls per run). tests/unit/scripts/test_classifier_regress_selftest.py exercises score_verdicts and CorpusCase parsing against a small FIXTURE corpus (no network, no real CLI) — verifies the gate arithmetic itself (accept-list honouring, per-cluster breakdown, exit-code decision) independent of live Haiku output. The real gate — `make classifier-regress` against tests/corpus/classifier/questions.json (≥100 labelled cases incl. the 7 measured-broken clusters from the 2026-07-03 simulation: recurring/check-in swallowed by digest #13/#43/#54/#57/#71, no job management #89/#90, chat-trap losses #50/#53, fragile digest-control #35/#91/#99, sub-threshold reminder reaching the root runner #78/#96) via the REAL ClaudeCliBackend, bounded concurrency=5 — is run manually: intent accuracy must be 100% (accept-lists honoured for labelled-ambiguous cases), intent+action+kind accuracy ≥99%; a failing gate exits non-zero and prints the per-cluster breakdown. Every free-text slot in the corpus's `expected`/`accept` fixtures is asserted verbatim (no translation, FR-12) against the real backend's distilled_payload. Documented as a MUST-run step in prompts/classifier.md's own CHANGELOG discipline (classifier.md:1-13 precedent) — a change without a fresh classifier-regress run must not be committed.

V-M-CLASSIFIER-RECURRENCE.evidence: parse_recurrence over a ru corpus: «каждый день в 9 (утра)» → Recurrence(kind='daily', time_hhmm='09:00', tz=user_tz); «по будням в 19:00» → kind='weekly', weekdays==(mon..fri), time_hhmm='19:00'; «еженедельно по понедельникам и пятницам в 8» → weekly, weekdays==(mon,fri), '08:00'; ambiguous / no time-of-day / monthly / interval / raw-cron-string → RecurrenceParseResult(recurrence=None, escalate=True, reason set); optional Haiku fallback path returns a parsed Recurrence when the rule-based regex misses and a backend+prompt are wired, else escalate. Recurrence.to_cron(): daily → {'hour': HH, 'minute': MM}; weekly → {'day_of_week': comma-joined 3-letter names in calendar order, 'hour': HH, 'minute': MM}; round-trips through model_dump(mode='json') (frozen, extra forbidden). tz defaults to Settings.default_user_tz when the caller passes none. (aisw-oqq; D-002; tech-spec §3.)

commands.cmd: uv run pytest tests/unit/wiki/test_runner.py tests/unit/wiki/test_runner_aggregate.py tests/unit/wiki/test_runner_on_event.py tests/unit/wiki/test_streaming.py tests/unit/wiki/test_acquire.py -q

V-M-WIKI-RUNNER.evidence: run_wiki_session: assemble_prompt (base+overlay+per-WIKI CLAUDE.md atomic write), WikiLockAdapter acquire, spawn `claude --output-format stream-json`, parse_stream_json events, hard timeout + kill-sequence (WikiRunnerTimeoutError), on_event callback (per-event delivery, exception swallow); aggregate_text across stream-json shapes; classify_line pure mapping; AsyncioSpawner default.

V-M-WIKI-LIFECYCLE.evidence: normalize_wiki_name Cyrillic→Latin ISO 9 + PascalCase + slug (D-008); WikiLifecycleManager owner-scoped create/lookup/list/soft_delete/restore with AntiSpamCapError + NearDuplicateMatch (Levenshtein ≤2) + TrashRetentionExpiredError (D-041); parse/render_frontmatter + managed/user-zone markers + load_template sha256 (D-039); preflight 5-step grounding → PreflightReport.

V-M-WIKI-SCOPE.evidence: resolve_query_scope: confident single-domain match (hint_match.is_confident, MIN_SCORE/MIN_MARGIN gates) with a matching wikis dir entry → ScopeDecision(kind='scoped', target_stem, target_path, top_score, margin); ambiguous (margin below threshold), empty catalog, text over MAX_FASTPATH_CHARS, or a confidently-matched stem missing from the wikis dir mapping (race: WIKI deleted) → ScopeDecision(kind='cross', target_stem=None, target_path=None); ScopeDecision is frozen (AttributeError on mutation). collect_layouts: renders "# Карта WIKI пользователя" + one "## <stem>" section per (stem, wiki_dir) pair sourced from each WIKI's managed CLAUDE.md zone (MANAGED_START/MANAGED_END); missing CLAUDE.md, absent markers, or an empty managed body all fall back to "(схема не описана)" per WIKI; per-WIKI excerpt capped at MAX_LAYOUT_CHARS_PER_WIKI; empty wikis sequence → "". Module is pure logic — no logging of its own; wiki.run.scope / wiki.run.scope.degraded are logged at the M-RUNTIME-WIRING call sites (aisw-o6m, ADR-034).

commands.cmd: uv run pytest tests/unit/inbox/test_materialize.py tests/unit/inbox/test_parser.py tests/unit/inbox/test_hint_cache.py tests/unit/inbox/test_hint_match.py tests/unit/inbox/test_staging.py -q

V-M-INBOX.evidence: inbox_wiki_path pure <wiki_root>/<telegram_id>/Inbox-WIKI (per-user media-staging root, D-022; aisw-12t); ensure_inbox_wiki idempotent first-contact materialise (D-004); extract_inbox_hint parses "## Inbox hint" body or None (D-016); InboxHintCacheRepo UPSERT/get/invalidate + get_or_refresh_hint stat→cache-hit on (size,mtime) else read+sha256; score_catalog token-overlap count (message ∩ each domain's hint+stem-name tokens, normalised: casefold word-runs, drop <3-char + stop-words) → ranked HintMatch; is_confident iff one domain has ≥MIN_SCORE(2) matched keywords AND ≥MIN_MARGIN(1) lead over #2 — single clear winner / ambiguous (gap too small) / weak winner / empty catalog / stem-name folding / token normalisation / threshold boundaries unit-covered (aisw-5sd); stage_media atomic write to _staging/<run_id>_<sha8>.<ext>; promote_to_raw / promote_path_to_raw atomic move to raw/media/<ISO8601>_<sha8>.<ext>; sweep_staging TTL delete (one inbox_root) + sweep_all_user_staging over every per-user Inbox-WIKI (D-022). aisw-xi8 (classifier v2, Phase-B, DEC-9): the formerly-private _tokens is promoted to a public tokens() one-line rename — same normalisation (lowercased word-runs, drop <3-char + stop-words), same return type (frozenset[str]); test_hint_match.py's existing assertions against the tokenizer behaviour keep passing unchanged (a pure rename, no logic change); scheduler.manage.match_jobs_by_needle imports and reuses it directly rather than duplicating the tokenizer.

V-M-INBOX-ROUTER.evidence: parse_router_reply: happy path for each RouterIntent (route/create_wiki/clarify/reject), multi-line notes, target_wiki null/empty → None, preamble + quoted example → last ```router block wins, missing block → RouterDecision(intent=CLARIFY, parsed_ok=False, notes=trimmed-original-or-generic), unknown intent value → fallback, ROUTE/CREATE_WIKI without target → demoted to CLARIFY, RouterDecision frozen + extra=forbid, RouterError is an Exception (aisw-dsg; prompts/inbox.md ≥1.1.0).

V-M-INBOX-ROUTE.evidence: resolve_target_wiki: CREATE_WIKI → new dir, created=True; idempotent second call → created=False; ROUTE existing → created=False; ROUTE missing → create + on_route_missing callback; AntiSpamCapError → RouteRejection("cap"); WikiNameError → RouteRejection("bad_name"). stage_raw_into_wiki: text → raw/<ts>_text.md body verbatim, media_rel=[]; voice/photo → .md sidecar with YAML front-matter + promote_path_to_raw into raw/media/ (StagedRaw.media_abs absolute & exists, source moved); already-gone media → skipped. pick_domain_overlay: domain-<slug>.md if present else domain-default.md. build_ingest_prompt references the raw path(s) + the user text (aisw-zd9; D-004). aisw-e45 (Phase-C): route_action_to_payload ∘ route_action_from_payload is identity for ROUTE and CREATE_WIKI decisions with/without media (RouterIntent/RouterDecision/Path reconstructed by type; missing media → []); from_payload raises ValueError on a missing 'decision' or a bad 'source'.

V-M-INGEST-IDEM.evidence: IdempotencyService.check_update_id (L1 tg_updates, 24h TTL), check_content (L2 seen_files, SHA-256, 30d TTL), record_dedup_choice (dedup_hits); normalize_text NFKC+strip+lower+collapse-ws for text hashing; compute_content_hash per ContentKind; SeenFileMatch on collision (D-018 amended 2026-05-10, INV-4).

V-M-OPS-PII.evidence: PIIRedactor tiered redaction (NIST SP 800-122) with HMAC secret + on/off toggles + make_structlog_processor; module-level redact convenience; purge_user GDPR endpoint admin-gated + allow-listed stores (D-034 §10.4); RETENTION_POLICIES matrix; run_purge with dry_run + cutoff_utc/oldest_kept_utc; register_retention_jobs adds §10.4 cron; purge_trash_sweep tier-1/tier-2 final sweep; purge_staging wraps inbox.staging.sweep_staging.

V-M-OPS-BACKUP.evidence: snapshot_databases VACUUM INTO state/snapshots/<UTC>/ for jobs/audit/sessions (0700 dirs); purge_old_snapshots 7d rolling retention; register_db_snapshot_job cron 03:00 UTC (D-037, INV-2/INV-3); restore test reloads a snapshot; init_wiki_git idempotent git init + .gitignore; format_commit_message "<job_id>(<category>): <title>"; auto_commit stage-all + commit, no-op when nothing staged; WikiGitError on CLI failure.

commands.cmd: uv run pytest tests/unit/tg/test_bot.py tests/unit/tg/test_middleware_auth.py tests/unit/tg/test_middleware_start_bypass.py tests/unit/tg/test_confirm.py tests/unit/tg/test_output.py tests/unit/tg/test_stream_edit.py -q

V-M-TG-TEXT.evidence: build_bot (HTML parse_mode) + build_dispatcher (AllowlistMiddleware outer); AllowlistMiddleware denies unknown telegram_id with DENY_TEXT_RU + auth.deny, /start bypass, carries user_record (D-031, D-042); ConfirmationService auto/implicit/explicit + 10min TTL + compute_payload_hash + 3-button keyboard (D-023); request_explicit accepts a keyboard_factory (default 3-button; build_route_confirm_keyboard → 2-button confirm/cancel for Inbox-WIKI route confirms, aisw-e45); deliver_output ≤3500 inline / ≤10000 ChainSplitter (i/M footer, ≤3 parts) / >10000 HaikuSummarizer→LengthCapSummarizer + RunOutput persistence (D-024, D-025); HtmlBalancer tag balancing; StreamEditor 1.5s/Δ≥50 throttle + chain-split at 4000 + final-flush on end/exception/cancel (D-026).

V-M-TG-VOICE.evidence: FasterWhisperTranscriber lazy CTranslate2 int8 CPU model, ru/en, RTF target ≤0.5, fallback lang ru, raises VoiceUnavailableError on missing faster-whisper; VoiceHandler stages bytes via inbox.staging.stage_media then transcribes → (MediaRef, Transcript) (D-022); audio + video_note route here from handlers.

V-M-TG-PHOTO.evidence: PhotoIngestor stages photo bytes to _staging/ via inbox.staging.stage_media → MediaRef; PHOTO_MIME_TO_EXT closed mapping of allowed photo MIME types; photos (and image-documents) run Claude vision via the wiki pipeline media_paths (D-022, tech-spec §9).

V-M-SCHEDULER-CRON-USER.evidence: create_cron_user_job INSERTs jobs.Job(kind='cron_user', status='scheduled', priority=Lane.CRON_WRITE, payload=CronUserPayload(recurrence:Recurrence, command:str, wiki_id:str|None)) and registers AsyncIOScheduler.add_job(fire_cron_user_job, CronTrigger(**recurrence.to_cron(), timezone=user_tz), args=[job_id], id=f'cron_user:{job_id}', replace_existing=True); commit-before-add_job; returns int job_id. fire_cron_user_job(job_id:int) loads Job, guards status=='scheduled' (vanished/wrong-status row → log scheduler.cron_user.fire.job_missing + return — idempotent on APScheduler replay), constructs CronUserQueueMsg(job_id, owner_telegram_id, chat_id, command, correlation_id=uuid4().hex, scheduled_at_utc=datetime.now(UTC)), pushes via queue.put(Lane.CRON_WRITE, msg), UPDATE status='queued'; raises CronUserContextNotInitialisedError when set_cron_user_context never called. Mirrors firing.py shape — module-level _ctx registry; picklable int callback for SQLAlchemyJobStore (aisw-02v; D-002; tech-spec §3 D-011). Check-in scheduling (aisw-xi8, Phase-B, DEC-6/DEC-8): create_check_in_job mirrors create_cron_user_job exactly — INSERT+commit a jobs.Job(kind='check_in', payload=CheckInPayload(question_topic, recurrence, wiki_id)) BEFORE scheduler.add_job(fire_check_in_job, CronTrigger(**recurrence.to_cron(), timezone=user_tz), args=[job_id], id=f'check_in:{job_id}', replace_existing=True); returns int job_id; scheduler.check_in.scheduled logged. fire_check_in_job(job_id:int): loads Job, guards status; builds CheckInQueueMsg(kind='check_in', job_id, owner_telegram_id, chat_id, question_topic, correlation_id=uuid4().hex, scheduled_at_utc=now_utc) → queue.put(Lane.CRON_WRITE, msg) (scheduler.check_in.fired) — NO CLI invocation happens here (execution is M-SCHEDULER-CONSUMER's concern, matching the cron_user producer/consumer split). test_check_in_producer.py mirrors test_cron_user_producer.py's shape (schedule → assert Job row + CronTrigger id; fire → assert queue receives the correctly-typed CheckInQueueMsg + status update).

V-M-SCHEDULER-CONSUMER.evidence: CronConsumer.run() drains PriorityJobQueue.get() in a single async loop (NFR-1 concurrency=1, at-most-once); per item: CronUserQueueMsg.model_validate raises ValidationError → log+continue; UPDATE jobs.Job status='running' + started_at_utc=now_utc; spawn subprocess via asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(*argv, stdout=PIPE, stderr=PIPE, cwd=neutral_cwd(claude_config_dir), env=build_env(claude_config_dir)) where argv = [resolve_binary(claude_binary), *system_prompt_argv(prompt_path), '--setting-sources','','--disable-slash-commands','--permission-mode','dontAsk','--disallowedTools','WebFetch','--', command] — the Claude CLI runs DIRECTLY, NO systemd-run wrapper (aisw-abc/ADR-010 simple single-user model; CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR via build_env, parity with M-WIKI-RUNNER); asyncio.wait_for(proc.communicate(), timeout=600); TimeoutError → kill_with_sequence(proc, grace_seconds=DEFAULT_TERM_GRACE_SECONDS) → status='failed' + reply '❌ Тайм-аут 10 минут.'; exit_code==0 → ChainSplitter().split(stdout.decode('utf-8','replace')) chunked bot.send_message * n_chunks + status='finished'; exit_code!=0 → '❌ Ошибка ({code}): '+truncate_stderr(stderr, limit=512) + status='failed'; TelegramAPIError on send → log scheduler.consumer.deliver_failed + status='failed' (no re-raise — loop survives). Cancellation: CancelledError propagates out of .run() so __main__ shutdown can await the task. CronUserQueueMsg Pydantic v2 discriminated union with discriminator='kind' (NFR-5) round-trips via model_dump_json. Runner-parity hardening (aisw-o3m): _build_argv now inserts --setting-sources "" / --disable-slash-commands / --permission-mode dontAsk / --disallowedTools WebFetch after system_prompt_argv and before the aisw-0j4 literal '--' + command separator (positional-prompt invocation model kept — no -p/stream-json switch); test_consumer.py asserts the flags are present and that the '--' guard still precedes a '-'-prefixed command, and that argv[0] is the claude binary with no systemd-run/--scope/--slice present (aisw-abc). (aisw-02v; aisw-abc; ADR-010; R-1 R-2 R-6 mitigations; D-011 §3; D-021). Check-in execution (aisw-xi8, Phase-B, DEC-8): CronUserQueueMsg.model_validate is replaced by parse_queue_msg (the widened QueueMsg discriminated union) so the SAME drain loop dispatches both kinds by their 'kind' discriminator — a per-kind branch on CheckInQueueMsg builds a Claude CLI invocation whose system prompt is assembled from prompts/check_in.md + msg.question_topic, instructing the model to generate exactly ONE ru question about that topic; happy path → the generated question sent via bot.send_message, status='finished'. On exit_code!=0 OR asyncio.TimeoutError (the same 600s wait_for/kill_with_sequence path as the cron_user branch) the consumer does NOT send the generic "❌ Ошибка (...)" line for this kind — instead it sends a deterministic ru fallback "Хотел спросить: {question_topic}" verbatim, logs scheduler.check_in.fallback (error_class, job_id), and still marks the row 'finished' (a delivered fallback is a completed check-in from the user's perspective, not a failure state — FR-6, R-5 mitigation: an elderly user is never left without the scheduled question). test_consumer_check_in.py: happy path asserts the sent text is the model's question (not the fallback line); CLI exit≠0 and CLI timeout both assert the exact fallback string + scheduler.check_in.fallback logged + status='finished' (not 'failed'); parse_queue_msg round-trips both CronUserQueueMsg and CheckInQueueMsg from the same PriorityJobQueue.get() call site (no separate queues, no consumer-side branching on queue identity — only on the validated message's discriminator).

V-M-TG-CRON-ADD.evidence: register_cron_add_handlers(router, get_user_tz) registers Command('cron_add'). Handler splits CommandObject.args on the first '|' (left=schedule_text trimmed, right=command trimmed). Cases: (a) no '|' OR empty schedule OR empty command → reply CRON_ADD_USAGE_RU + CRON_ADD_USAGE_RU_HINT, log tg.command.cron_add.usage; (b) parse_recurrence(schedule_text, user_tz=await get_user_tz(owner_telegram_id)) returns RecurrenceParseResult(escalate=True, reason=...) → reply CRON_ADD_USAGE_RU_HINT, log tg.command.cron_add.escalate reason=...; (c) parsed Recurrence → create_cron_user_job(...) → int job_id → reply '✅ Запланировано (id={job_id}): {_humanize_recurrence(rec)}. Команда: {command_preview}', log tg.command.cron_add.parsed + tg.command.cron_add.scheduled job_id=...; (d) defensive try/except around the create+answer wrapper → reply _GENERIC_ERR_RU + log tg.command.cron_add.failed error_class=... (handler never bubbles to the dispatcher). _humanize_recurrence pure: daily → 'каждый день в HH:MM ({tz})'; weekly → 'каждую {ru weekday list} в HH:MM ({tz})'; monthly → 'каждое {day_of_month}-е число в HH:MM ({tz})'. (aisw-02v; R-4 mitigation: parser-escalate becomes user-visible usage hint, no silent bad-cron).

V-M-SCHEDULER-MANAGE.evidence: NEW module (DEC-9, aisw-xi8, Phase-B). list_owner_jobs(session, owner_telegram_id): returns only enabled user-facing kinds (reminder_job|recurring_reminder|check_in|digest|cron_user); purge and wiki_run rows for the same owner are EXCLUDED (system-internal, never user-visible) — each returned row is rendered via humanize_recurrence for cron-shaped jobs and a local-time string for once-shaped jobs; owner isolation asserted (another owner's jobs never appear). match_jobs_by_needle(jobs, needle): casefold whole-token overlap scoring reusing the promoted inbox.hint_match.tokens() helper — 0 jobs match → empty result (caller renders "не нашёл" + the list); exactly 1 job scores >0 and strictly above the rest → single match; ≥2 jobs tie or multiple score >0 → all returned ranked (caller builds the job_pick disambiguation keyboard); an empty/whitespace-only needle matches nothing (never "everything", fail-safe against an accidental mass-disambiguation). cancel_job(scheduler, session, job): scheduler.remove_job(_job_key(job.kind, job.id)) is called before the status UPDATE; a job whose APScheduler entry is already gone (race) does not raise — remove_job's JobLookupError is caught and logged, the DB row is still marked 'cancelled' (idempotent on retry). reschedule_once(scheduler, session, job, new_when_utc): scheduler.reschedule_job(key, trigger=DateTrigger(new_when_utc)) — verified against apscheduler 3.11.0 semantics (replaces ONLY the trigger, preserves job id/args, recalculates next run; Context7 unreachable this session, verified via WebFetch against apscheduler.readthedocs.io/en/3.x/userguide.html + the in-repo firing.py:665 reschedule_job precedent) — Job.scheduled_at_utc updated to match; a past new_when_utc is rejected by the SAME parse_time validator the create-path uses (caller's responsibility, not manage.py's). reschedule_recurring(scheduler, session, job, new_recurrence): scheduler.reschedule_job(key, trigger=CronTrigger(**new_recurrence.to_cron())) + the persisted payload's recurrence field rewritten and re-validated through parse_job_payload (round-trips); closes the measured #35/#91/#99 digest-control defect cluster (digest reschedule now works, resolved Q3). _job_key(kind, id): pure mapping — 'reminder_job'→'reminder:{id}', 'recurring_reminder'→'recurring:{id}', 'check_in'→'check_in:{id}', 'digest'→'digest:{id}', 'cron_user'→'cron_user:{id}' — asserted to match the literal id= strings already used at firing.py:217/:613 and cron_user.py:121 (SSoT, no drift between the two call sites). No module-level context registry (unlike firing.py/cron_user.py) — every function takes its AsyncSession + AsyncIOScheduler as explicit params, since this module is called synchronously from the tg request path, never from a picklable APScheduler callback.
